question: Two forces $\bar{F}_1$ and $\bar{F}_2$ are acting on a body. One force has magnitude thrice that of the other force and the resultant of the two forces is equal to the force of larger magnitude. The angle between $\vec{F}_1$ and $\vec{F}_2$ is $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)$. The value of $|n|$ is _____.
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \left|\vec{F}_1\right|=\mathrm{F} \\ & \left|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}_{\mathrm{R}}\right|=\left|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}_2\right|=3 \mathrm{~F} \\ & \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{R}}^2=\mathrm{F}_1^2+\mathrm{F}_2^2+2 \mathrm{~F}_1 \mathrm{~F}_2 \cos \theta \\ & 9 \mathrm{~F}^2=\mathrm{F}^2+9 \mathrm{~F}^2+6 \mathrm{~F}^2 \cos \theta \\ & \cos \theta=-\frac{1}{6} \\ & \theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{-6}\right) \\ & \mathrm{n}=-6 \\ & |\mathrm{n}|=6\end{aligned}$

The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
solution:
Time period of oscillation is given by T=2πlg , here, l is length and g is acceleration due to gravity.

From above relation, we have  g=14π2T2l.

Fractional error in g is stated as 

Δgg=2ΔTT+Δll

⇒Δgg×100=2×1100×0.5×100+1 mm10 cm×100

⇒Δgg×100=5100×100=5%

Hence, value of x=5.

ρ=Rπr2l

For percentage error, we can write Δρρ=ΔRR+2Δrr+Δll

=10100+2×0.050.35+0.215×100

=110+27+175×100

Hence, Δρρ=39.9%

question: Two resistance are given as R1=(10±0.5) Ω and R2=(15±0.5) Ω. The percentage error in the measurement of equivalent resistance when they are connected in parallel is
solution:
For a parallel combination the equivalent resistance is 

1R=1R1+1R2   ...(i)

A general expression for error can be written as x±∆x

Differentiating equation (i)

dRR2=dR1R12+dR2R22   ...(ii)

The equivalent resistance with R1=10 Ω & R2=15 Ω is

1R=110+115

⇒R=15025=6Ω

Substituting the above value in equation (ii)

dR=360.5100+0.515×15=0.26

⇒R=6±0.26

Hence, the percentage error is

q: In an experiment the values of two spring constants were measured as $k_{1}=(10 \pm 0.2) \mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}$ and $k_{2}=(20 \pm 0.3) \mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}$. If these springs are connected in parallel, then the percentage error in equivalent spring constant is :
question: In an experiment the values of two spring constants were measured as $k_{1}=(10 \pm 0.2) \mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}$ and $k_{2}=(20 \pm 0.3) \mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}$. If these springs are connected in parallel, then the percentage error in equivalent spring constant is :
solution:
For parallel combination: $k_{eq} = k_1 + k_2 = 10 + 20 = 30$ N/m
For sum, absolute errors add:
$\Delta k_{eq} = \Delta k_1 + \Delta k_2 = 0.2 + 0.3 = 0.5$ N/m
Percentage error $= \frac{\Delta k_{eq}}{k_{eq}} \times 100 = \frac{0.5}{30} \times 100 = 1.67\%$

q: In an experiment to measure focal length $(f)$ of convex lens, the least counts of the measuring scales for the position of object $(\mathrm{u})$ and for the position of image $(\mathrm{v})$ are $\Delta \mathrm{u}$ and $\Delta \mathrm{v}$, respectively. The error in the measurement of the focal length of the convex lens will be:
question: In an experiment to measure focal length $(f)$ of convex lens, the least counts of the measuring scales for the position of object $(\mathrm{u})$ and for the position of image $(\mathrm{v})$ are $\Delta \mathrm{u}$ and $\Delta \mathrm{v}$, respectively. The error in the measurement of the focal length of the convex lens will be:
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{f}^{-1}=\mathrm{v}^{-1}-\mathrm{u}^{-1} \\ & -\mathrm{f}^{-2} \mathrm{df}=-\mathrm{v}^{-2} \mathrm{dv}-\mathrm{u}^{-2} \mathrm{du} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{df}}{\mathrm{f}^2}=\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{v}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{u}^2} \\ & \mathrm{df}=\mathrm{f}^2\left[\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{v}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{u}^2}\right]\end{aligned}$

q: If a wire is stretched to make it $0.1 \%$ longer, its resistance will :
question: If a wire is stretched to make it $0.1 \%$ longer, its resistance will :
solution:
$\mathrm{R} \propto \ell^2$ (for a given volume)
$$
\Rightarrow \frac{\Delta \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{R}} \%=\frac{2 \Delta \ell}{\ell} \%
$$
Thus when wire is stretched by $0.1 \%$ resistance increases by $0.2 \%$
